title: carlina vulgaris subsp. intermedia
intro: Carlina vulgaris subsp. intermedia, also known as the intermediate hawk's beard or carline thistle, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is characterized by its tall stem and distinctive purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Carlina vulgaris subsp. intermedia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herb for maintaining overall health and well-being. Traditionally, the plant is prepared through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
